title: ChangeGate Evaluation Event v1
description: Sink-agnostic product event envelope for ChangeGate merge-eligibility decisions and their real-world outcomes (JSONL, SQLite, or remote sinks). Digest-and-reference only. Digests use the repository's ONE canonical representation 'sha256:' + 64 lowercase hex, exactly as produced by agent_core.build_harness.canonical.canonical_digest(). Per-event conditional constraints enforce the causal chain (evaluation -> attempt -> completion -> validation -> rollback, plus feedback targeting an exact prior event), so a minimal common-envelope-only instance is invalid for every event type and a merge result can be deterministically paired with its attempt. Reference values use a narrow bounded identifier grammar: no whitespace, path separators, traversal, URLs, or content-bearing structures. SCOPE LIMIT (spec §21): schema-level controls cover shape, grammar, length and forbidden content-bearing structures ONLY; redaction, secret scanning, allowlist validation and access policy remain application-sink duties. This schema is NOT a secret scanner: it does not and cannot identify every possible secret value, and no such claim may be made for it. Defined by docs/strategy/CHANGEGATE_VERTICAL_MVP_SLICE_1_POLICY_SPEC.md §19/§21. Status: DRAFT_FOR_OWNER_REVIEW; no production emitter exists yet.

noWhitespace.comment: Anchors alone are not enough: in ECMA/Python regex '$' also matches before a trailing newline, so 'abc\n' would satisfy '^[a-z]+$'. Every string grammar below additionally forbids ANY whitespace character.

opaqueRef.description: Bounded opaque identifier: letters, digits, '_', '-', '.' only. Rejects whitespace, newlines (including a trailing newline), path separators, '..' traversal, URL schemes and diff/code fragments. Covers the existing generated-id grammar [a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,63}. A defensive deny-list rejects the most common credential shapes; it is NOT a secret scanner (see the schema description).

eventRef.description: Reference to another event's event_id. Distinct from content-bearing values: an event reference may only ever name an event.

canonicalDigest.description: THE canonical digest representation: 'sha256:' + 64 lowercase hex characters (agent_core.build_harness.canonical.canonical_digest output). Bare hex, uppercase, other algorithms, wrong length and any whitespace are invalid.

gitCommitSha.description: Dedicated Git object field: 40 (sha1) or 64 (sha256) lowercase hex characters. Structured values get their own field instead of being embedded in an opaque string.

taskRef.description: The ONE task-reference grammar (existing TaskContract.task_id grammar) reused by event.task_ref AND event.decision_ref.task_ref, so no weaker nested grammar can admit whitespace/newlines a top-level field rejects.

event_id.description: Globally unique event identifier (generated by the emitting application layer, never by the pure evaluator).

event_type.description: Closed v1 event vocabulary linking evaluation to merge/CI outcome, rollback/rework, and feedback. Review/approval/exception events are NOT part of Slice 1A: per accepted OD-S1A-009 they belong to a future governance slice gated on OD-S1A-005, and no event may carry a second policy-decision identity.

occurred_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp supplied by the emitting application layer.

task_ref.description: Task identifier (the one shared taskRef grammar; current TOMTIT task ids stay representable). Identity equality rule (spec §19.5): for every event, event.task_ref MUST equal event.decision_ref.task_ref. JSON Schema cannot express cross-field equality, so the artifact causal validator enforces it and rejects any mismatch.

subject_ref.description: Domain-neutral subject reference shaped after ADR-003 §8 SubjectRef (a JSON reference shape only; it does not implement the future kernel SubjectRef model). Structured values use dedicated fields: 'kind' is a closed machine-readable enum and a Git commit goes in 'commit_sha', never inside 'value'.

digest.description: Identity equality rule (spec §19.5): when kind is git_candidate and this digest is non-null it MUST equal event.decision_ref.candidate_digest; the artifact causal validator enforces the equality.

context_digest.description: Canonical digest of the deterministic policy input (A2 input_digest) for evaluation events. Identity equality rule (spec §19.1): for CHANGEGATE_EVALUATION_COMPLETED it MUST equal event.decision_ref.input_digest; the artifact causal validator enforces the equality.

decision_ref.description: Reference to the merge-eligibility decision this event is about, carrying the full deterministic decision identity (decision + input + policy-record digests) and the task/candidate it was made for, so downstream events can be checked for lineage. Referencing only: an event can never author or amend a decision.

evaluation_event_ref.description: event_id of the CHANGEGATE_EVALUATION_COMPLETED event this event descends from.

attempt_event_ref.description: event_id of the exact CHANGEGATE_MERGE_ATTEMPTED event a completion resolves — this is what makes multiple attempts deterministically pairable with their result.

merge_event_ref.description: event_id of the exact CHANGEGATE_MERGE_COMPLETED event a validation or rollback refers to.

validation_event_ref.description: event_id of the CHANGEGATE_POST_MERGE_VALIDATION event that motivated a rollback, where applicable.

target_event_ref.description: For feedback: the exact prior event whose real-world result is being judged.

target_event_type.description: For feedback: which kind of thing the human is judging (eligibility decision, merge outcome, validation, rollback).

evidence_refs.description: Evidence involved, by identifier and digest only — never evidence content.

outcome.description: Observed result object. Never empty: a required outcome carries at least a status and a machine-readable detail code.

resulting_commit_sha.description: For a merge completion: the commit the merge actually produced.

feedback.description: Structured human feedback about a decision or a real-world outcome: actor + verdict + category code + optional reason code + optional opaque redacted-comment digest. No raw feedback text is representable. Feedback is a recorded signal, never ground truth, and never mutates the active policy (spec §20); this envelope has no policy-mutation field.

actor_ref.description: The human/source identity giving the feedback. Distinct from provenance.emitter, which identifies the emitting application.

comment_digest.description: Digest of any free-text comment; the raw comment is stored, redacted, outside this envelope.

provenance.description: Who EMITTED this event (the application), and from which trace context. This is never the human/source identity of a feedback actor.

privacy_classification.description: Mandatory privacy class; sinks must refuse an event without one (spec §21).
